entry.title: Re: Job-Specific Competencies Logic in PM Form
entry.content:
Hi @tekassf 

There is no way to control this as of now in system.

You need to make sure the employee is having correct job code during the form launch time.

entry.title: Re: Custom report : Stock In‑Out & Balance Report with Tr...
entry.content: Thanks for your reply and the information provided. I still have the following questions: Since the transaction currency is different from the company code currency, all amounts involve exchange rate conversion and result in exchange rate differences even if Material Ledger is activated with multiple currency types. Therefore, the customer's requirement is to calculate amounts directly in the transaction currency. Currently, we plan not to calculate the step-by-step rolling moving average price. Instead, we will calculate the moving average price for the selected query period using the following formula: Periodic Weighted Average Price = (Opening Amount + Current Period Receipt Amount) / (Opening Quantity + Current Period Receipt Quantity) I would like to ask the following questions: What is the underlying CDS view used to retrieve the inventory quantity on a specific date in the Material Inventory Values - Balance Summary query you provided? Can we use the CDS view I_MaterialStockTimeSeries to get the inventory quantity on a specific date? If we calculate the periodic material average price using a custom formula, can we create a new calculated field in the query to define the calculation logic and store the result? If not, how should we implement the formula calculation and store the calculated values? thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: Document transfer from RCM to Onboarding 2.0
entry.content: Yes, you can do that via CPI. I have done that in one of the projects in 2025.

entry.title: Re: T code:MB5M -ALV Grid option enabling
entry.content:
Perform some search on the optional p_grid parameter created by 1792526 - Performance of MB5M - Display of shelf life list 

  • BAdI MM_UI_OPTIMIZATIONS of Enh. sport ES_MM_IM_DBSYS_OPT (required to display the parameter)

entry.title: Re: Simplifying HTML5 Content Consumption Across Subaccounts
entry.content:
Hi,

I’m currently following your approach, and I’ve successfully exposed my HTML5 app from the provider subaccount and made it visible in the SAP Build Work Zone of the consumer subaccount.

However, when I try to open the app, I encounter an error (see screenshot). The app fails to load via the HTML5 App Runtime, and it shows a message indicating that the app resource might be temporarily unavailable or moved.
Thanks in advance for your guidance.

Update:

During testing, I initially encountered an issue where the app could not be loaded via the HTML5 App Runtime (error indicating the resource might be unavailable). After further investigation, I was able to resolve this by configuring the destination to use the exact Work Zone URL of the provider subaccount.

However, up to now, I’m facing a new issue: when opening the app, the browser throws a security error — “Refused to display in a frame because it set 'X-Frame-Options' to 'sameorigin'.”

So currently, the app is visible and reachable, but cannot be rendered inside Work Zone due to this restriction.

entry.title: Re: Requirement for Project-wise Customer and Supplier Line Item Report
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM - Health Monitoring
entry.content:
We have configured SAP Cloud ALM for Health Monitoring, and it is working as expected. We have total of 30 systems. 

At present, we need to navigate to each individual system to review the overall system health and monitoring checks. We would like to know if there is a way to create or access a centralized dashboard that displays data across multiple systems based on specific KPIs/metrics, rather than reviewing each system separately.

Could you please advise if this is possible and, if so, share the recommended approach? Can we connect to SAP Cloud ALM to PowerBI to create dashbaord?

entry.title: New transaction type or movement code created - how to handle this in breakdown categories , FS ?
entry.content:
HI, 

A new transaction type or movement code has been created in S4 

272 - Cash movements of interest derivatives

This transaction type should be reflected in SAP S4 GR in breakdown categories as the existing TT 270. 

What kind of change and where should be done to reflect this new TT as business requests the following :

I believe in the past XXX to do something also with the breakdown categories in order for the new movement code to be used in combination with certain accounts. This new movement code "272" can be set up in the same way as movement code "270". 

 

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Which BADI is used to add sold to party bp in conditi...
entry.content: Hi Lakshmipathi, this isnt working in our case. BADi - APOC_ADD_CONDITION_PARAMETER is not available in system which i am using. and as per ABAP team BADI - APOC_ADD_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS is used only in case of email o/p so if we add sold to party here, this is not going to apply for print output.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
Re: "Entre the MRP Type" error while replication of mater...
entry.content: Hi Binoy, MRP group is an optional field and doesnt have any mandatory field requirements. It is neither assigned automatically to the plant. I believe you mean MRP type set up as part of MRP Profile. MDG sends the data to both private and public environment and both have similar configurations. But only while sending the data to private we are getting this error.

entry.title: Re: Restricting Storage Location in MIGO posting
entry.content:
For your requirements, you may consider leveraging BADI MB_MIGO_BADI, specifically the CHECK_ITEM or CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE methods.

  • What restrictions do you intend to implement?

    • Please specify the business rules or validation logic that woumd require custom checks at the item level.
  • Why are Standard Authorization Objects not sufficient

entry.title: Re: Automatic Warehouse Task Creation at GR Flag not working
entry.content:
Do you already checked KBA such as

  • 3435676 - Putaway Task is not created automatically for inbound deliveries with customizing field "Automatic WT" - SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • 3688859 - Automatic WTs are not created after GR even if it is customized in ST level

entry.title: I am facing issue with creating the SEPA mandate. Message no SEPA181
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,
I am trying to create a SEPA mandate but it is giving me error as Mandate already exist as SEPA181 . 

But the existing one is CANCELLED status.


What should i check to get rid of this error?
Is there any flag which should be marked as True to make this create?
Please guide me through this.

Thanks in advance Gurus,
Nipun

entry.title: Re: Blog#1: Finally it is here - How "pony" brings custom fields to SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi @gregorw,
our custom fields are always just added below the standard fields. This way, a change done by SAP should not break the UI.
We actually just had the proof that our approach works. Maybe you have noticed that SAP introduced new partner functions in the Feature a few weeks ago (Developer, Tester etc.). We had already our custom fields available at that point for the Feature. When the new standard fields were available, our custom fields were just pushed a bit more down in the screen. Nothing was required to change from our side. The user was able to just continue working.

entry.title: Get started with Cloud ALM
entry.content:
We are currently preparing for the migration from SAP Solution Manager to Cloud ALM. Could you please provide us with some reference documents for the monitoring scenarios, especially for Job Monitoring in Cloud ALM?

Specifically, we would like to know what exact content can be monitored. Additionally, are there any guides available that can help us get started with the configuration? Thank you.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Get started with Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello,

Details are available at Transition to SAP Cloud ALM.

In addition, about the Job Monitoring, please refer to the following document: SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Expert Portal

Job & Automation Monitoring

  • Overview
  • Content
  • Setup & Configuration
  • How-To Videos

Thank you.

entry.title: latest release of sap S/4 HANA database on suse 15

entry.title: Re: Intercompany COst (Expense) Allocation and Accrual -...
entry.content:
Hello @Paresh_Dalvi59,

Thank you for your question.

In the context of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Professional Services, the scenarios you described around cross-company resource deployment and accrual handling are common in complex project environments. However, it is important to understand how standard system capabilities align with these requirements.

For the first requirement, which relates to cross-company expense allocation, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud does not provide a native mechanism to directly replicate or transfer purchase order–based expenses posted in one company code (Company Code A) into a project structure maintained in another company code (Company Code B). In other words, expenses incurred via procurement in Company Code A cannot automatically appear as project costs in Company Code B simply through standard MM postings.

That said, SAP does support cross-company cost recognition using intercompany service processes. The recommended standard approach is to leverage intercompany activity allocation or intercompany billing. In this setup, the contingent worker in Company Code A is treated as a resource providing services to the project in Company Code B. Through activity allocation or intercompany billing, the cost of these services is transferred and posted to the project in Company Code B. This enables proper project cost visibility, while also ensuring financial transparency and compliance with intercompany accounting principles. However, this approach works best for service-based scenarios (e.g., time or effort-driven costs) and not for direct mirroring of supplier invoice or purchase order expenses.

Regarding the second requirement, which involves accrual adjustment against actuals, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud provides standard functionality through the Accrual Engine and Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR). These tools allow Company Code B to recognize expected costs and post accruals based on planned or forecasted project data. However, there is no standard functionality that directly links accrual postings in Company Code B with actual expense postings occurring separately in Company Code A.

For accruals to be automatically adjusted based on actuals, those actual costs must first be visible within the same project structure in Company Code B. This is where intercompany processes again play a critical role. If intercompany billing or activity allocation is used to transfer costs from Company Code A to Company Code B, the project in Company Code B will receive actual costs. Once actuals are available, Event-Based Revenue Recognition can automatically compare planned versus actual values and adjust accruals accordingly. This provides a standard, integrated mechanism for accrual reconciliation—but only after costs are brought into the project via intercompany processes.

In summary, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud does support the overall business requirement, but not in the exact form of direct expense replication across company codes. Instead, it requires a design aligned with standard intercompany service flows. Direct PO-based cost mirroring and automatic cross-company accrual matching are not available in standard and would require either manual adjustments or side-by-side extensions using SAP BTP if absolutely necessary.

Therefore, the recommended approach is to model the scenario using intercompany activity allocation or billing, ensuring that costs are posted into the receiving company code’s project. This will allow standard accrual and revenue recognition functionalities to operate effectively and maintain accurate financial reporting across entities.

Hope this answers your query.

entry.title: Balance Sheet/Income Statement - Multidimensional - Not assigned Ledgers
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud and using the Balance Sheet / Income Statement – Multidimensional Report.

I have created a Financial Statement Version (FSV) for P&L reporting and assigned all relevant P&L G/L accounts properly.

However, in the report output, I can see a section called “Not Assigned”, which includes Balance Sheet G/L accounts. This is confusing for end users because the report is intended only for P&L reporting.

Is there any standard way to:

  • Hide Balance Sheet accounts from this report, or
  • Remove/suppress the “Not Assigned” section, or
  • Can you suggest any other tile for better P&L Report. 
  • Configure the report to show only P&L accounts based on FSV?

 

Attached Picture for your reference. 

 

Kindly suggest the best practice or standard solution in S/4HANA Private Cloud.

Thanks & Regards,

silambarasan R.

entry.title: Requirement for Reporting Purchase Order Item Revision Level
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We have a new requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud regarding the display of the current revision level for Purchase Order line items.

We could not identify any standard report or application that displays the current revision level of a Purchase Order item.

As part of our analysis for a custom report, we reviewed the CDS view I_PURCHASEORDERITEMAPI01; however, the revision level field is not available in this CDS view.

We then attempted to create a custom CDS view by associating I_PURCHASEORDERITEMAPI01 with I_PRODUCT, where the revision level field is available. However, the revision level is not being populated, even though it is maintained for the corresponding material.

Kindly advise on a suitable approach or alternative solution to fulfill this requirement.

entry.title: Re: France: Register test participant ID with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
entry.content:
Without changing anything and without getting any feedback from SAP on my ticket, I simply tried again to register the Peppol ID's in the DRC - using the same SIREN numbers ....

... and surprise: Now it worked out without problems !

I have no idea if the problem was at SAP or not, but this has been a very annoying experience.

entry.title: What's the best Practice for SAP ERP Integration with Third-Party E-Invoicing Platform via APIs?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am currently working on a project involving SAP ERP integration with a third-party e-invoicing platform.
The solution will support both inbound and outbound e-invoices and communicate with the external platform through REST APIs.

The planned solution includes:

  • Outbound sales invoices generated in SAP and sent to the third-party platform through APIs.

  • Inbound purchase invoices received from the external platform and processed within SAP.

  • ALV reports with actions such as Send, Refresh Status, Reprocess, and View Logs.

  • Custom Z-tables for audit trails, statuses, acknowledgments, and API logs.

  • Comprehensive error handling, monitoring, and reprocessing capabilities.

 

From an ABAP architecture perspective, I would like to understand the best practices for implementing such a solution.
My question is; What's the best method or technology to implement this integration? Should I use classic REST (CL_HTTP_CLIENT) or use Odata services or ... etc?

I would appreciate hearing about real-world implementations, lessons learned, architectural recommendations, and common pitfalls to avoid.

Thank you in advance for your insights.

entry.title: Re: Storage Location (SLoc) Field Not Editable on Main De...
entry.content: Check SAP note 3566956 addresses your requirement.  By the way, when you post, please see that you also add in which environment you are working like Public Cloud or Private Cloud based on which, solution might differ

entry.title: Re: Provision users from Microsoft EntraID to SAP Cloud Identity Services
entry.content:
Hi I know this post is a bit older.

Im looking for some help. 

I managed to get the users from entra id to cis. Im using an extra EntraID Group (CIS Test) for that. All Users in that group should be provisioned to the CIS. 

Now im having trouble to allow those Users SSO to Ariba. 

Users which where created in CIS are able to connect via SSO to Ariba. Users created via Entra are not.
I know its a configuration think and hope someone can point me to the solution.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Creation of new access group
entry.content:
Hello 

it is not possible to add a new Access group in SAP Business ByDesign.

Regards,
Harshal

entry.title: Difference Between AA Reports and GL Reports – AUC BS accounts
entry.content:
We identified a discrepancy between values shown in Asset Accounting (AA) reports and General Ledger (GL) account reports for Assets Under Construction (AuC). After investigation, the difference appears to be related to down payments that are posted to a technical account according to the current configuration.

Issue Description

  • In AA reports, the full amount is displayed under the balance sheet GL account assigned to the AuC.
  • In GL reporting, the postings are shown differently and appear correctly based on the actual posted accounts.
  • This creates a mismatch between AA report balances and GL account report balances.

Investigation and Findings

Our analysis shows that the discrepancy is caused by down payment transactions that are posted to a technical account, as defined in system configuration. In AA reporting, these values are reflected as part of the AuC balance sheet GL account, whereas in GL reporting they remain visible in the technical account where they were actually posted.

Observed Behavior

entry.title: Integration suite API that retrieves the message size
entry.content:
Hello

We need a standard SAP Integration Suite API that allows us to retrieve the size of the sent payload.

Currently, we are using the API at https://api.sap.com/api/MessageProcessingLogs, but it doesn't provide the size value.

Can you tell us if this is the correct API or what procedure we should follow to extract the payload size?

Our idea was to create a Groovy step within iFlow that adds a custom property to the log for the size. Please confirm or deny if this would be the correct approach.

entry.title: Change of billing material determined in project billing
entry.content:
We face regularly the situation that the employee records time using a specific activity type, but when it comes to billing the desire is not to use the billing material determined in project billing, but instead use a different billing material. 

Use cases:

  • Junior Hours are recorded, but should be billed as Senior hours
  • Hours are recorded as non billable, but should be billed

For consistency in output generation and pricing, we would need to get the correct material. 

How can this be achieved?

BR

Johannes

entry.title: Coffe Corner - It's kind of hard to find my blogs in SAP Comunity.
entry.content:
It's kind of hard to find my blogs in SAP Community. 😞

I now found this again, so I am happy. But it took some time.
https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/exploring-sql-features-using-a-demo-report/ba-p/13320434

Searchterms for the future:
ABAP SQL Select Demo


Notes:
- not part of blogs.sap.com
- no way(right?) to just "show me all my blogs"
- there is a red note on on it ( "We have launched new Developer forums/groups in the SAP Community. If you are here to publish developer- or SAP-technology related blog posts, please check out our new groups instead. You can find more information about the developer forums in this What's New post." )
-> kind of indicating something is wrong / not good...

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content: Great solution

entry.title: Implementing Excel-Based Bulk Data Upload with Template Download in SAP Fiori Elements List Report
entry.content:
Implementing Excel-Based Bulk Data Upload with Template Download in SAP Fiori Elements List Report

Overview

This guide demonstrates how to implement Excel Template Download and Bulk Data Upload functionality in a Fiori Elements List Report application using RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) in SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

Features covered:

  • Download an Excel template with predefined column headers
  • Upload Excel file with data to create multiple records
  • Client-side file handling with UI5
  • Server-side Excel parsing using XCO library

Prerequisites

  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment or SAP S/4HANA (RAP enabled)
  • SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)
  • Basic knowledge of RAP, CDS, and Fiori Elements
  • Existing RAP Business Object with List Report application

Step 1: Create Abstract Entity for File Parameters

Create an abstract entity to handle the file upload parameters passed from the UI to the backend.

@EndUserText.label: 'Abstract entity for Excel file parameters'
define abstract entity ZAE_EXCEL_FILE_PARAM
{
    mimeType    : abap.string(0);
    fileName    : abap.string(0);
    fileContent : abap.rawstring(0);
}

Note: This entity defines the structure for passing file metadata (MIME type, file name) and the actual file content (as base64 encoded raw string) from the frontend to the backend.

Step 2: Add Actions in Behavior Definition

Add static actions for download and upload functionality in your behavior definition file.

managed implementation in class zbp_i_travel unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for ZI_TRAVEL alias Travel
persistent table ztravel
draft table ztravel_d
etag master LastChangedAt
lock master total etag LastChangedAt
authorization master ( global )
{
  // ... existing configurations ...

  // Static actions for Excel operations
  static action downloadTemplate;
  static action uploadExcel parameter ZAE_EXCEL_FILE_PARAM;

  // ... other actions ...
}

Add method definitions in your behavior implementation class:

CLASS lhc_travel DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS:
      downloadTemplate FOR MODIFY
        IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION Travel~downloadTemplate,
      
      uploadExcel FOR MODIFY
        IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION Travel~uploadExcel.
ENDCLASS.

Step 3: Configure UI Extension in Business Application Studio

3.1 Connect OData Service and Create List Report

  1. Open your project in SAP Business Application Studio
  2. Connect your OData V4 service
  3. Generate a List Report application using the Fiori Elements template

3.2 Add Controller Extension

  1. Open Page Map view in BAS

  2. Navigate to your List Report page

  3. Find "Controller Extensions" section for your page

  4. Click "Add Controller Extension"

  5. Provide a controller name (e.g., ExcelHandler)

     

3.3 Add Custom Actions via Page Editor

  1. Go to Application Info page

     

  2. Click on List Report to open Page Editor

     

  3. In Page Editor, navigate to: Table → Toolbar → Actions

  4. Click "Add Custom Action" and configure:

    For Download Template Button:

    Property Value
    Action IDdownloadTemplateAction
    Button TextDownload Template
    Handler FileSelect your controller extension
    Handler MethoddownloadTemplate

    For Upload Excel Button:

    Property Value
    Action IDuploadExcelAction
    Button TextUpload Excel
    Handler FileSelect your controller extension
    Handler MethoduploadExcelDialog
     

Step 4: Implement Controller Extension (Frontend)

4.1 Controller Extension Code

Create/update the controller extension file:

webapp/ext/controller/ExcelHandler.controller.js

sap.ui.define([
    'sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension',
    'sap/ui/export/Spreadsheet',
    'sap/m/MessageBox',
    'sap/m/MessageToast',
    'sap/ui/core/Fragment'
], function (ControllerExtension, Spreadsheet, MessageBox, MessageToast, Fragment) {
    'use strict';

    return ControllerExtension.extend('yournamespace.ext.controller.ExcelHandler', {
        
        override: {
            onInit: function () {
                // Initialize service path - adjust according to your service
                this.oServicePath = "/YourEntitySet/com.sap.gateway.srvd.your_service.v0001.";
            },
            
            editFlow: {
                onAfterActionExecution: function (oEvent) {
                    // Handle action execution based on action name
                    if (oEvent.split(".")[6] === 'downloadTemplate') {
                        this.downloadTemplate();
                    }
                    if (oEvent.split(".")[6] === 'uploadExcel') {
                        this.uploadExcelDialog();
                    }
                }
            }
        },

        /**
         * Download Excel Template
         * Generates an Excel file with column headers for data entry
         */
        downloadTemplate: function () {
            // Define columns for the template
            var aColumns = [
                {
                    label: 'Travel ID',
                    property: 'TravelID',
                    type: 'String',
                    width: 15
                },
                {
                    label: 'Agency ID',
                    property: 'AgencyID',
                    type: 'String',
                    width: 15
                },
                {
                    label: 'Customer ID',
                    property: 'CustomerID',
                    type: 'String',
                    width: 15
                },
                {
                    label: 'Begin Date',
                    property: 'BeginDate',
                    type: 'Date',
                    width: 12
                },
                {
                    label: 'End Date',
                    property: 'EndDate',
                    type: 'Date',
                    width: 12
                },
                {
                    label: 'Booking Fee',
                    property: 'BookingFee',
                    type: 'Number',
                    scale: 2,
                    width: 12
                },
                {
                    label: 'Total Price',
                    property: 'TotalPrice',
                    type: 'Number',
                    scale: 2,
                    width: 12
                },
                {
                    label: 'Currency Code',
                    property: 'CurrencyCode',
                    type: 'String',
                    width: 12
                },
                {
                    label: 'Description',
                    property: 'Description',
                    type: 'String',
                    width: 40
                },
                {
                    label: 'Status',
                    property: 'Status',
                    type: 'String',
                    width: 12
                }
            ];

            // Configure spreadsheet settings
            var oSettings = {
                workbook: {
                    columns: aColumns
                },
                dataSource: [{}], // Empty data for template (headers only)
                fileName: 'Travel_Upload_Template.xlsx',
                showProgress: false
            };

            // Generate and download Excel file
            var oSheet = new Spreadsheet(oSettings);
            oSheet.build()
                .then(function () {
                    MessageToast.show("Template downloaded successfully!");
                })
                .catch(function (oError) {
                    MessageBox.error("Error generating template: " + oError.message);
                })
                .finally(function () {
                    oSheet.destroy();
                });
        },

        /**
         * Open Upload Dialog
         * Creates and displays a dialog for file selection
         */
        uploadExcelDialog: function () {
            var oView = this.getView();
            var that = this;

            if (!this.oDialog) {
                this.oDialog = Fragment.load({
                    id: oView.getId(),
                    controller: this,
                    name: "yournamespace.ext.fragment.FileUploadDialog"
                }).then(function (oDialog) {
                    oView.addDependent(oDialog);
                    return oDialog;
                });
            }

            this.oDialog.then(function (oDialog) {
                oDialog.open();
            });
        },

        /**
         * Handle File Selection
         * Reads and stores selected file content
         * @param {sap.ui.base.Event} oEvent - File change event
         */
        onFileChange: function (oEvent) {
            var oFile = oEvent.getParameters().files[0];
            
            if (!oFile) {
                return;
            }

            // Store file metadata
            this.fileType = oFile.type;
            this.fileName = oFile.name;

            // Read file content as Base64
            var oFileReader = new FileReader();
            oFileReader.onload = function (oLoadEvent) {
                // Extract Base64 content (remove data URL prefix)
                this.fileContent = oLoadEvent.target.result.split(",")[1];
            }.bind(this);

            oFileReader.readAsDataURL(oFile);
        },

        /**
         * Handle Upload Button Press
         * Sends file content to backend for processing
         */
        onUploadPress: function () {
            var that = this;
            this.oFileUploaderInput = this.getView().byId("idFileUpload");

            // Validate file selection
            if (!this.fileContent) {
                MessageBox.error("Please select a file to upload");
                return;
            }

            // Get OData model
            var oModel = this.base.getModel();

            // Create action binding context
            var oContext = oModel.bindContext(this.oServicePath + "uploadExcel(...)");

            // Set action parameters
            oContext.setParameter("mimeType", this.fileType);
            oContext.setParameter("fileName", this.fileName);
            oContext.setParameter("fileContent", this.fileContent);

            // Execute action
            oContext.execute()
                .then(function () {
                    MessageToast.show("Excel uploaded successfully!");
                    that._closeDialogAndReset();
                    // Refresh list to show new data
                    that.base.getExtensionAPI().refresh();
                })
                .catch(function (oError) {
                    MessageBox.error("Upload failed: " + oError.message);
                    that._closeDialogAndReset();
                });
        },

        /**
         * Handle Cancel Button Press
         * Closes the upload dialog
         */
        onCancelUpload: function () {
            this.getView().byId("idFileUploadDialog").close();
        },

        /**
         * Helper: Close dialog and reset file input
         * @private
         */
        _closeDialogAndReset: function () {
            this.oDialog.then(function (oDialog) {
                oDialog.close();
            });
            if (this.oFileUploaderInput) {
                this.oFileUploaderInput.clear();
            }
            this.fileContent = '';
        }
    });
});

4.2 Create Upload Dialog Fragment

Create a fragment file for the upload dialog:

webapp/ext/fragment/FileUploadDialog.fragment.xml

<core:FragmentDefinition 
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns:f="sap.ui.layout.form"
    xmlns:u="sap.ui.unified">
    
    <Dialog id="idFileUploadDialog" title="Upload Excel Data">
        <VBox id="idVBox" width="100%" class="sapUiSmallMargin">
            
            <f:SimpleForm 
                id="idSimpleForm" 
                editable="true" 
                layout="ResponsiveGridLayout" 
                maxContainerCols="2">
                <f:content>
                    
                    <Label 
                        id="idFileUploadLabel" 
                        required="true" 
                        text="Choose Excel File"/>
                    
                    <u:FileUploader 
                        id="idFileUpload"
                        name="excelFileUpload"
                        change="onFileChange"
                        width="100%"
                        style="Emphasized"
                        fileType="xls,xlsx"
                        placeholder="Select Excel file (.xls, .xlsx)"
                        tooltip="Upload Excel file with travel data"/>
                    
                </f:content>
            </f:SimpleForm>
            
            <MessageStrip
                text="Please use the Download Template option to get the correct format for upload."
                type="Information"
                showIcon="true"
                class="sapUiSmallMarginTop"/>
            
        </VBox>
        
        <footer>
            <Toolbar id="idFooterToolbar">
                <ToolbarSpacer/>
                <Button 
                    id="idUploadButton"
                    text="Upload"
                    type="Emphasized"
                    press="onUploadPress"
                    icon="sap-icon://upload"/>
                
                <Button 
                    id="idCancelButton"
                    text="Cancel"
                    press="onCancelUpload"
                    icon="sap-icon://cancel"/>
            </Toolbar>
        </footer>
        
    </Dialog>
    
</core:FragmentDefinition>

Step 5: Implement Backend Logic (ABAP)

5.1 Define Table Type for Excel Data

Add the following type definitions in your behavior implementation class:

CLASS lhc_travel DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
  
    " Type definitions for Excel parsing
    TYPES: 
      lty_c20  TYPE c LENGTH 20,
      lty_c40  TYPE c LENGTH 40,
      lty_c100 TYPE c LENGTH 100,
      
      BEGIN OF ty_excel_upload,
        travel_id    TYPE lty_c20,
        agency_id    TYPE lty_c20,
        customer_id  TYPE lty_c20,
        begin_date   TYPE string,    " Will be converted to date
        end_date     TYPE string,    " Will be converted to date
        booking_fee  TYPE string,    " Will be converted to decimal
        total_price  TYPE string,    " Will be converted to decimal
        currency_code TYPE lty_c20,
        description  TYPE lty_c100,
        status       TYPE lty_c20,
      END OF ty_excel_upload,
      
      tt_excel_upload TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_excel_upload WITH EMPTY KEY.

    " Constants for messaging
    CONSTANTS:
      c_msg_id     TYPE symsgid VALUE 'ZTRAVEL_MSG',
      c_msg_number TYPE symsgno VALUE '001'.

    METHODS:
      downloadTemplate FOR MODIFY
        IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION Travel~downloadTemplate,
      
      uploadExcel FOR MODIFY
        IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION Travel~uploadExcel.
        
ENDCLASS.

5.2 Implement Upload Method

CLASS lhc_travel IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD downloadTemplate.
    " Template download is handled on the frontend
    " This method can be used for logging or additional backend processing if needed
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD uploadExcel.
    DATA: lt_excel       TYPE tt_excel_upload,
          lt_data_create TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE zi_travel,
          lv_file_content TYPE xstring,
          lv_index       TYPE i.

    "---------------------------------------------------
    " 1. Get and Validate File Content
    "---------------------------------------------------
    lv_file_content = keys[ 1 ]-%param-fileContent.

    IF lv_file_content IS INITIAL.
      APPEND VALUE #(
        %msg = new_message_with_text(
                 severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                 text     = 'No file content provided. Please select a file.' )
      ) TO reported-travel.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    "---------------------------------------------------
    " 2. Parse Excel File Using XCO Library
    "---------------------------------------------------
    TRY.
        " Create document reader from file content
        DATA(lo_document) = xco_cp_xlsx=>document->for_file_content( lv_file_content )->read_access( ).
        
        " Get first worksheet
        DATA(lo_worksheet) = lo_document->get_workbook( )->worksheet->at_position( 1 ).

        " Define selection pattern (skip header row, start from row 2)
        DATA(lo_selection_pattern) = xco_cp_xlsx_selection=>pattern_builder->simple_from_to(
          )->from_row( xco_cp_xlsx=>coordinate->for_numeric_value( 2 )
          )->get_pattern( ).

        " Read data into internal table
        lo_worksheet->select( lo_selection_pattern
          )->row_stream(
          )->operation->write_to( REF #( lt_excel )
          )->execute( ).

      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_excel_error).
        APPEND VALUE #(
          %msg = new_message_with_text(
                   severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                   text     = |Excel parsing error: { lx_excel_error->get_text( ) }| )
        ) TO reported-travel.
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.

    "---------------------------------------------------
    " 3. Clean and Validate Data
    "---------------------------------------------------
    " Remove empty rows
    DELETE lt_excel WHERE 
      travel_id   IS INITIAL AND
      agency_id   IS INITIAL AND
      customer_id IS INITIAL.

    IF lt_excel IS INITIAL.
      APPEND VALUE #(
        %msg = new_message_with_text(
                 severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-warning
                 text     = 'Excel file contains no valid data rows.' )
      ) TO reported-travel.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    "---------------------------------------------------
    " 4. Map Excel Data to RAP Create Structure
    "---------------------------------------------------
    lv_index = 0.

    LOOP AT lt_excel INTO DATA(ls_excel).
      lv_index += 1.

      " Convert string values to appropriate types
      DATA(lv_begin_date) = CONV d( ls_excel-begin_date ).
      DATA(lv_end_date)   = CONV d( ls_excel-end_date ).
      DATA(lv_booking_fee) = CONV decfloat34( ls_excel-booking_fee ).
      DATA(lv_total_price) = CONV decfloat34( ls_excel-total_price ).

      APPEND VALUE #(
        %cid = |CID_{ lv_index }|
        
        %data = VALUE #(
          AgencyID     = ls_excel-agency_id
          CustomerID   = ls_excel-customer_id
          BeginDate    = lv_begin_date
          EndDate      = lv_end_date
          BookingFee   = lv_booking_fee
          TotalPrice   = lv_total_price
          CurrencyCode = ls_excel-currency_code
          Description  = ls_excel-description
          Status       = ls_excel-status
        )
        
        %control = VALUE #(
          AgencyID     = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
          CustomerID   = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
          BeginDate    = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
          EndDate      = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
          BookingFee   = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
          TotalPrice   = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
          CurrencyCode = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
          Description  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
          Status       = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
        )
      ) TO lt_data_create.
    ENDLOOP.

    "---------------------------------------------------
    " 5. Create Records Using EML
    "---------------------------------------------------
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_travel IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Travel
      CREATE FIELDS (
        AgencyID
        CustomerID
        BeginDate
        EndDate
        BookingFee
        TotalPrice
        CurrencyCode
        Description
        Status
      )
      WITH lt_data_create
      MAPPED DATA(lt_mapped)
      REPORTED DATA(lt_reported)
      FAILED DATA(lt_failed).

    "---------------------------------------------------
    " 6. Handle Results and Return Messages
    "---------------------------------------------------
    IF lt_failed-travel IS NOT INITIAL.
      " Report failures
      LOOP AT lt_failed-travel INTO DATA(ls_failed).
        APPEND VALUE #(
          %cid = ls_failed-%cid
          %msg = new_message_with_text(
                   severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                   text     = |Failed to create record: { ls_failed-%cid }| )
        ) TO reported-travel.
      ENDLOOP.
    ELSE.
      " Success message
      APPEND VALUE #(
        %msg = new_message_with_text(
                 severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-success
                 text     = |Successfully created { lines( lt_mapped-travel ) } records.| )
      ) TO reported-travel.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Project Structure Summary

your-project/
├── src/
│   ├── zae_excel_file_param.ddls.asddls    # Abstract entity
│   ├── zi_travel.bdef.asbdef                # Behavior definition
│   ├── zbp_i_travel.clas.abap               # Behavior implementation
│   └── ...
│
└── webapp/
    ├── manifest.json
    └── ext/
        ├── controller/
        │   └── ExcelHandler.controller.js    # Controller extension
        └── fragment/
            └── FileUploadDialog.fragment.xml  # Upload dialog

Additional Resources

  • SAP RAP Documentation
  • XCO XLSX Library Reference
  • Fiori Elements Controller Extensions
  • sap.ui.export.Spreadsheet API

Conclusion

This implementation provides a complete solution for Excel-based bulk data operations in Fiori Elements applications. The approach leverages RAP’s static actions for clean separation of concerns and uses standard SAP libraries for reliable Excel handling.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
entry.content: @SAP/cds/common

entry.title: Re: Week 4: AI Developer Challenge SAP-RPT-1 Model
entry.content:
I completed the SAP-RPT-1 challenge and identified the data inconsistencies that were influencing the prediction results.

To achieve the correct sales group predictions, I adjusted the inconsistent feature values in the training dataset so that records belonging to the same product type followed a consistent pattern. After correcting those cells and re-running the inference in the SAP-RPT-1 Playground, the model successfully predicted the expected sales groups for the products with missing target values.

This exercise was a great demonstration of how data quality and feature consistency directly impact the performance of foundation models for structured enterprise data. It also provided valuable hands-on experience with SAP-RPT-1's approach to tabular classification tasks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Dimension Tables Move to the File Repository
entry.content:
@Max_Gander Great news.

Is the SAC feature linked to Public Dimensions in Shared Spaces in Datasphere?
Or is SAC leading the way for Datasphere in this regard?
In most scenarios, data is pulled from Datasphere as live models. It would be helpful if public dimensions could be shared across Datasphere Spaces.

entry.title: Re: Coffe Corner - It's kind of hard to find my blogs in SAP Comunity.
entry.content:
Oh, happy to learn that I was wrong:
as of now, it IS quite easy to see all my / ones Blogs Posts:

1. https://community.sap.com/t5/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/3837
2. Tab Blog Posts.
(3. There you go! - Maybe hit "+ view more" a few times...)

Awsome!
(This is expected behavior. Just in case you wonder, why I am so happy: I'm sure this was not always the case within the last few years... )

entry.title: Re: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content: Could you share the relevant section from the server build log (cfgsrv.log or sqlsrv.log)? Also check whether there are any leftover ASE processes, port conflicts, or old SYBASE environment variables from the previous installation. These are common causes of the server build phase hanging during configuration.

entry.title: Experience Modernized Enterprise Integration – Onsite!
entry.content:
After the latest wave of exciting announcements at SAP Sapphire 2026, you may still be buzzing from all the innovative technologies that lie ahead of us. But most of you probably want to see these innovations in action - interactive, hands-on, and with real people onsite.

Luckily, for everything related to Modernized Integration, we have something in store for you: the Integration Modernization AI Infodays in Germany. If you are based in Germany or located nearby, you can register for one of three onsite events and learn how to Accelerate Innovation with AI and Modern Integration.

Register here: https://events.sap.com/ai-driven-integration/en_us/home.html 

The Integration Modernization campaign turns inspiration into execution - showing how SAP Integration Suite operates as the control plane for Agent Governance: the backbone where APIs, events, identities, policies, and observability converge to safely orchestrate human and AI agents at scale.

We translate vision into actionable architecture by modernizing legacy middleware, establishing API- and event-first design principles, and operationalizing guardrails for AI and agent workloads.

Attendees will learn how to:

  • Understand new integration patterns such as Agent-to-System and Agent-to-Agent
  • Explain the MCP Reference Architecture
  • Use the MCP protocol to connect and govern agent capabilities in a policy-aware way
  • Easily consume APIs with consistent security, throttling, and lifecycle controls via SAP Integration Suite
  • Automate workflows (e.g., with n8n) to build event-driven, composable solutions - accelerating innovation while staying within enterprise guardrails
  • Understand identity setup across integration components (e.g., API keys and SAP IAS)

With SAP Integration Suite providing secure connectivity, policy enforcement, and end-to-end monitoring, you'll reduce technical debt, increase delivery velocity, and move decisively toward an autonomous enterprise.

After SAPPHIRE comes the opportunity to get your hands dirty - join us on the journey.

If you are interested please refer to this registration page to secure your spot: https://events.sap.com/ai-driven-integration/en_us/home.html

Stay tuned!

entry.title: Re: How to Display Position Code in Latest People Profile Header – SAP SuccessFactors EC Workaround
entry.content: Great workaround! This is a practical solution for organizations that rely heavily on position codes for HR operations and reporting. The latest People Profile provides a cleaner UI, but the loss of quick visibility to the position code can impact consultant and HR efficiency. I appreciate how you've leveraged configurable fields to meet the business requirement without enabling all external codes globally. Thanks for sharing a clear and user-friendly approach.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
Service Contracts with Negative Values

Thanks, 
Camille

entry.title: Transport route
entry.content:
Hi SAP. We have a case as the following:

  • 3-tier landscape
    • Dev: 100
    • Qas: 100, 200, 300
    • Prod: 100
  • Transport movement is as following:
    • Dev.100 : Create transport
    • QaS.200 and QaS.300: Import the transports
    • After it is tested successfully in QaS.200 and QaS.300, we move the transports further to QaS.100 and Prod.100

I'd like to hear how would you suggest to set up the transport route for this scenario. I understand this is not directly a Cloud ALM topic, but it's relevant because we want to set up a route for Cloud ALM TMS management.

entry.title: Re: Credit Management for Professional Services Project B...
entry.content:
Hi @IsmaelMateos 

Thank you for your question.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is no standard integration between FSCM Credit Management and Project Billing Requests (PBR) for Customer Projects. Credit checks are executed only at SD document level (e.g., billing/invoice), not during PBR creation or processing. [community.sap.com]

Implications:

  • You cannot block Project Billing Request creation or processing based on credit limits or overdue amounts.
  • You can apply credit checks at invoice stage (Billing Document), where FSCM Credit Management can block or require release.
  • For earlier control, use workflow (Manage Project Billing Request) to introduce approval before billing.

 Conclusion: Use a combination of SD credit checks (for invoice blocking) and workflow approvals (for PBR control)—this is the standard approach in Public Cloud.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Connection between SAC Analytics Cloud and SAP HANA C...
entry.content:
Hi @Stephany429 ,

Although this might sounds strange, the native connection to import data from HANA Cloud is on the roadmap for Q3 release. This is planned to be rolled-out mid-August. You can find more information here: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=67838200100800006884&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=A51713C4AB2F1EDF8FD544918325686E

Alternative is to expose your calculation view as an OData service and use the generic OData option in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Kind regards,
Martijn van Foeken | Interdobs

entry.title: sap RAR POB Cancellation Process /SD Return Order
entry.content:
Dear Community

We are using OCM and OIP with RAR version 2.0 and I'm not sur how standard SAP handles POB cancellation in the context of return Orders from SD.

We are sending a Reason from Rejection as part of the SD Return order and seeing that the FINAL_DATE is getting populated on the SDOI item (resulting from the return) in table FARR_D_ORD_MI, but not in the POB table itself FARR_D_POB. 

My question is how does standard SAP marks the POB as cancelled / completed in the context and whether program FARR_POB_CANCELLATION is relevant for our RAR settings and release.
I couldn't find an OSS note that might fix a possible system bug; any hint on what need to be checked, methods, classes, BRF function is appreciated.
thanks

Karim

Hi,

I have the same issue. Have you been able to find a solution?

Alternatively, were you able to remove the task from the employee profile? It is currently showing on the employee's homepage, and I am not sure how to remove it.

Thanks,

Edy

entry.title: Re: FI12创建银行账户信息，无法保存
entry.content:
多半是因为忘了进到“开户行连接”里添加House Bank。

小半是因为做了上面这步，但返回GUI后忘了保存进TR。

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
Re: Initial load failed - WFA on Hana
entry.content:
Hello @YSE-1 

Yes, the issue was properly explained. I also added the link to this post so they have full details and context. They went through this post and stated that they are not the ones to address this issue. 

I also got a call from the support team this morning, and they reemphasised that they are not the ones to fix this issue and to bring the issue back here. Please see the communication sent after the call this morning 

 

 

Hello,

Thank you for your time, as per our communication if you are not able to access the link, you can post it in the communication where you communicated with the team before and explain to them that support only assist with Functional issue listed below, however all implementation question are to be posted to WFA on HANA Expert either in community or Expert page.

  List of  functional queries:

  • Navigating in WFA portal
  • WFA permissions
  • User Maintenance
  • Exporting reports
  • Custom Reports (Canvas/ORD)
  • Investigate/Query Workspace
  • Schedule reports
  • Headlines/tiles
  • DTD
  • Tree Security

Best regards,

Jean-Claude

SAP Support

entry.title: Maximum Aggregated External Receipts
entry.content:
Hello, 

We wish to implement a Supplier Constraint, for a group of references in a supplier. 

The supplier tells me he can provide X quantity of the group of references, no specifying the mix, that can be up to us.

How can I enter this aggregated Constraint? 

Currently the Maximum External Receipts could be an option but it's defined product level, so not really answering to my need. Could the creation of an "Maximum Aggregated External Receipt" help? This is not currently on the standard.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
entry.content: 5479f071efa9abc07e657e1e1c13f1b7561901b710bf817e583bb4f691995d99

entry.title: Integration approach for existing (“green”) SmartRecruiters customers
entry.content:
Hello community,
 
We are currently supporting several clients who are existing SmartRecruiters customers (“green” clients), and who are now planning to implement SAP SuccessFactors (Employee Central and potentially Onboarding).
 
At this stage, we understand that these customers are not eligible for the new productized integrations / standard connectors being released in 2026 as part of the SAP–SmartRecruiters partnership. We therefore need to define an alternative integration approach based on custom integrations. Our objective is to replicate the main integration flows typically covered by the productized integration, including:
- User Sync (creation, update, deactivation)
- Foundation / Organizational Data Sync (e.g., Departments, Locations, Legal Entities, etc.)
- Job Sync / Job Creation (from SAP SuccessFactors or external initiation)
- Hire Sync (from SmartRecruiters to Employee Central and potentially SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding)
 
In this context, we would like to understand:
 
1. What is the recommended integration approach for such “green” customers today?
2. Are there supported API-based patterns or reference architectures that we should follow?
3. Are there any known limitations or constraints compared to the productized integration? 4. Are there preferred tools or approaches (e.g., middleware, specific APIs, SCIM for users, etc.)?
5. Are there any existing implementation examples or best practices that can be shared? We are currently responding to active RFPs and need to propose a reliable and supported architecture to our clients. Any guidance or documentation would be highly appreciated. Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Plant language change from EN to JA ( Japanese) for JP00
entry.content: Kindly advise on the feasibility of changing the plant language in SAP from English to Japanese and outline the associated impacts.

entry.title: SAP Business One 9.3 HANA – System Extremely Slow After HANA Server Restart
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are facing a recurring issue in our SAP Business One 9.3 for SAP HANA environment. Our landscape consists of a SAP HANA Database Server and SLD Server on SUSE Linux, with the SAP Business One Application Server on Windows Server 2019. For the last few days, the company database is intermittently not visible on the SAP Business One login screen, requiring a restart of the HANA server to restore connectivity. After the restart, users can log in, but the system continues to run extremely slowly, affecting all business operations. This issue occurs almost daily and is impacting productivity. Kindly assist in identifying the root cause and provide recommendations for resolving the performance and connectivity issues at the earliest. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: VDA Automotive Flat File Processing with B2B Integration Factory
entry.content:
It looks like something is missing for scenario  Converting VDA Flat File to XML.

Since VDA doesnt have XSD, we can create a custom type.

entry.title: Re: End-to-End Forecasting with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content: This is a nice post. Since we are also working on such scenarios, this post was very helpful. Thanks @ilya_kirzner

entry.title: Re: Why Is My Content Marked as Spam?
entry.content:
Hi @AnnePetteroe!

My blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/running-claude-code-on-sap-generative-ai-hub-sap-ai-core-via-litellm/ba-p/14409432 was marked as spam after doing a change in a screenshot after releasing it.

May I request to release this blog so I can publish it?

Kind regards,

Nata.

entry.title: Re: ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code: Your Questions Answered
entry.content: @manish_kn  how do you connect to an on prem system without SSO? can you please share the example ?

entry.title: Re: How to Display Position Code in Latest People Profile Header – SAP SuccessFactors EC Workaround
entry.content:
@HarshPatelV ,
Thanks for the feedback! Glad you liked it—it’s a small tweak, but it makes a big difference in day-to-day usage.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Integration into Joule
entry.content: @GMI- We tried the setup with joule booster standard plan.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Dimension Tables Move to the File Repository
entry.content:
Hi @CGuerke,

No, this has no particular relation to seamless planning. It is purely about the way SAP Analytics Cloud dimension tables are managed - regardless whether they are stored in SAP Analytics Cloud or SAP Datasphere.

The cross-space usage of such dimensions for seamless planning has mostly been elaborated by our DEV teams. Likely, we won't be able to execute the development in time for a delivery in 2026 though. 

Kind regards
Max

entry.title: Re: CAnnot configure Microsoft SharePoint Adapter for SAP...
entry.content: Hi, but that is the problem. I cannot open the iflow.

entry.title: Re: Programm 6PP2 nicht anlegbar
entry.content:
Hallo Frau Häußler,

Mittels Transaktion OKS7 wird die Berichtsgruppe aus dem Mandanten 000 importiert. Eventuell ist diese Berichtsgruppe auch im Mandanten 000 nicht vorhanden oder schadhaft?

Ist die Berichtgruppe auf einem anderen System vorhanden? Dann könnten Sie versuchen diese auf diesem System mittels Transaktion GR57 als Datei zu exportieren (Kennzeichen "mit abhängigen Objekten " sollte gesetzt sein!) und dann auf einem andweren System mittels Transaktion GR58 zu importieren. Eventuell funktioniert dieses Vorgehen?

Mit freundlichen Grüßen

entry.title: Re: Need to activate scope item 1S4 for daily exchange rates
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Need to activate scope item 1S4 for daily exchange rates
entry.content: If your business requires daily automated exchange rate updates, activating scope item 1S4 is the correct approach. However, plan for additional subscription costs on SAP BTP for the Market Rates Management service and the chosen data provider.

entry.title: Re: End-to-End Forecasting with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Hii Ilya

I don't understand how you can write "data no longer needs to be physically copied out of BW" when you also writes "I used a Replication Flow to perform an initial load from a BW aDSO table into a local table". As I see it you must copy data into the object store, so unless your data already lives in the object store then it is a copy of data. Read more about it here: Customer-Managed Data Products in SAP BDC: What Re... - SAP Community

With kind regards

Kristian

entry.title: Re: ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code is now available on the VS Code marketplace!
entry.content: @Slobodchikov  @rafael_ali_terc71  Thanks for your feedback! Sorry, that ARM support for Windows does not work already. We discussed this topic in our internal sync today and we will look into how we can make this support possible.
